mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55 percent during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive return frequency above 55 percent over 60-day horizons in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which directly boosts short-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth rates correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 50 percent of observations during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.5 percent signals weakening aggregate demand and contracting corporate profit margins, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.5 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of instances when GDPC1 growth drops below the 1.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a positive return frequency below 50 percent over 60-day horizons in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation readings above 3.5 percent force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5 percent threshold during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production capacity utilization improve corporate operating leverage and profit margins, which translates directly into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency falls below 52 percent during periods of rising industrial output.
